tiers.label: Foundations
tiers.description: Core distributed building blocks every backend engineer needs.

tiers.label: Coordination & Shared State
tiers.description: Keeping many nodes in sync without a single point of failure.

tiers.label: Advanced Architectures
tiers.description: Production patterns behind checkout, Kafka, Stripe, and Dynamo.

tiers.label: Capstone
tiers.description: Multi-region and orchestration systems at production grade.

projects.title: URL Shortener
projects.description: Convert long URLs into short codes and redirect users back. Multiple API servers must generate unique short codes without colliding, and reads must stay fast under heavy traffic.
keyLearnings: Distributed unique ID generation without a single point of failure
Cache-aside pattern (check cache → fallback to DB → populate cache)
Stateless service design so any server can handle any request
Read-heavy vs write-heavy system design tradeoffs

projects.title: Rate Limiter as a Service
projects.description: A shared rate-limiting layer that enforces request limits consistently across many stateless API servers, so a user can't bypass limits by hitting different servers.
keyLearnings: Why local in-memory rate limiting fails in distributed setups
Atomicity and race conditions in shared counters
Token bucket vs sliding window tradeoffs
Designing for low-latency shared state checks on every request

projects.title: Job Scheduler / Background Workers
projects.description: An API instantly accepts a task and hands it to background workers that scale independently and recover from crashes without losing or duplicating work.
keyLearnings: Backpressure when workers can't keep up

projects.title: Distributed Cache with Invalidation
projects.description: Multiple app servers keep a local/shared cache. When the database changes, all caches must be invalidated or updated together — otherwise servers serve stale data.
keyLearnings: Why cache invalidation is one of the hard problems in CS

projects.title: Real-Time Chat / Notifications
projects.description: Users connect via WebSocket to different gateway servers. A message from Server A must reach a recipient on Server B, with presence tracking for who's online.

projects.title: Distributed File Storage
projects.description: Store large files reliably by splitting them into chunks, replicating each across storage nodes, and tracking locations in a metadata service so files survive node failure.
keyLearnings: Separation of metadata and data planes
Chunking large files for parallel read/write

projects.title: Leader Election + Service Discovery
projects.description: In a cluster of identical workers, exactly one acts as leader at any time. Other nodes detect failure and elect a new leader automatically.
keyLearnings: Leases and heartbeats for failure detection
Consensus tools vs building your own

projects.title: E-Commerce Order System (Saga)
projects.description: Checkout spans Inventory, Payment, and Shipping. If Payment fails after stock is reserved, the system rolls back with compensating actions instead of a single distributed transaction.
keyLearnings: Why 2PC doesn't scale well in microservices

projects.title: Mini Kafka
projects.description: An append-only log split into partitions, each replicated across brokers with a leader/follower model, plus consumer groups that track their own read offsets.
keyLearnings: Consumer group semantics and offset management

projects.title: Distributed Payment Processor
projects.description: A payment API that never double-charges even when clients retry after timeouts, and reliably publishes downstream events exactly once in practice.
keyLearnings: Outbox pattern for reliable event publishing

projects.title: Mini DynamoDB / Cassandra
projects.description: A key-value store that shards with consistent hashing, replicates keys, and uses quorum reads/writes plus conflict resolution to stay available during failures.
keyLearnings: Consistent hashing and data movement on scale

projects.title: Raft Consensus
projects.description: From-scratch Raft: leader election, log replication, and safety guarantees so a cluster agrees on one source of truth through crashes and partitions.
keyLearnings: Log replication and commit index tracking

projects.title: Multi-Region Active-Active
projects.description: Simulate geographic regions with async replication. Clients choose strong consistency (cross-region quorum) or eventual consistency (fast local reads).
keyLearnings: APIs that expose consistency as a knob

projects.title: Mini Container Orchestrator
projects.description: A control plane that tracks worker nodes, schedules containers by resource availability, detects failure, and reschedules workloads — the core K8s reconciliation loop.

projects.title: Distributed Lock Manager
projects.description: Distributed locks with lease expiration and fencing tokens — fixing the classic bug where a paused client still thinks it holds a lock and corrupts data.
keyLearnings: Why network mutexes fail (partial failures, GC pauses)
Fencing tokens for the zombie-client problem
Safety vs liveness in distributed locking
